content.answer:
For KR managed malls, benchmark is their peer group, meaning we make
   managed brands to spend at least to their peer group’s medianTR which is
   30%. Then on average these sellers will need to increase TR by <1% overall
   so this seems doable. How we do it : we give sellers this peer group median
   TR target, give them one quarter to achieve this TR, and if they fail to do
   so then the next quarter we’ll deduct the gap TR% from their escrow. We
   already ran this concept with UST brands during T2T meetings for the past
   few weeks and their main questions were “are our peer brands spending this
   much? Can we get this data for internal approval? Can you also take into
   account our offline & offsite spending.” So I think this is a
   difficult-but-feasible and good way to motivate sellers’ spending for SHP
   conversion and guarantee certain TR%

Why we benchmark peer group is because this is how most KR malls
      actually strategize, as Korean brands have similar branding and are
      competitors to each other

Why we don’t benchmark CODB of TTS is because out of the 11 UST
      managed brands brands I had T2T meetings with this year-end, all except 1
      brand’s plan next year is to cap or shrink TTS GMV due to its thin margin
      and 2 brand among them even closing TTS shop due to margin, so
it’s not the
      proper benchmark for for KR

For KR unmanaged brands/resellers, the benchmark is also their peer
   group. KR reseller average paid ads TR is 2.2% so making these sellers do
   mandatory ads at 2%. Unlike managed mall, cannot apply median because
   median TR is 0% and irrelevant. How we do it : we use the mandatory ads
   program to uplift the topline using it as tool to push for reseller SIP
   adoption as well (TR increasing due to mandatory ads so more pushed to do
   SIP and not pay the non-SIP penalty)

-> Having said that, with systematic programs in place for ‘26, KR can
achieve “even better” MP PC2 + SLS PnL $ vs. the scenario if we follow the
commission increase of CNCB, and below is the proposal with its impact for
your reference. In the very worst case if Legal team says such escrow
deduct for managed malls’ TR% program and mandatory ads program are not
doable in Korean regulatory environment, then we’ll re-propose with
commissions

[image: image.png]

-> Summary: KR proposes NOT to increase base commission but run
KR-customized TR improvement and ads program and aim KR ‘26 topline to
reach yearly 82k ADO (+40% YoY vs. 2025,  which is +20% YoY higher than the
commission increase only scenario). In terms of MP PC2 + SLS PnL, it will
be increased by $11.8M(vs ‘25) reaching yearly $35.6mil which Program only
case will generate “more” PnL of $0.9mil(+2.51% increase) vs. commission
only case. Also overall TR% reaching 18.97% and PC2% 2.59%(overall PnL %
6.17%)

[image: image.png]

================================================================================================

 For Non-Mall + Beauty, across most markets, there is probably room to
close the gap vs CNCB. The question is how to do it.

 I need to understand what the framework governing the thinking works first

   -

   Then I need a solution for KR Mall, KR Non-Mall, JP Non-Mall.

Do you think we can go with +2% in commissions and +2% in compulsory ads
take rate for JPCB?

-> For

Competitor CODB - for JP sellers in other CB EC PLT is known to be
   roughly 3%p higher vs. SHP

Hi Ian and all,

Please find SIP weekly update attached for review.

Addressing follow-up questions on AME launch from last weekly below
(detailed updates in slides)


   -

   Brunei - Aligned with MY AME Launch PMO
   -

      [P0] CNSIP / CN Direct: Jan-26
      -

      [P1] Local SIP / Local Direct: Feb-26
      -

      [P1] KRJP Direct: Feb-26 tentative; MY PMO + MYCB + KRJP team to
      finalize plan


For local sip/direct, i thought it was going to be Jan'26?


Jan’26 P0 segments are 1) MY local shops; 2) CNCB MP shops; 3) CNSIP shops
on MY platform to enable their fulfillment from MY/CN to BN.


For IDBN SIP and IDBN Direct, we target to be ready together with P0
segments by end-Jan, if not latest by 15 Feb to be ready for Raya ramp-up.
Key launch prep actions include logistics solution and channel setup;
global seller center + global wallet testing (for direct); HQ legal
review/modification of TOS.


Pls refer to slide 16 for overall timeline.


[image: image.png]


   -

   Cambodia & Laos - Aligned with VN/TH AME Launch PMO
   -

      [P0] VN-Cambodia / TH-Laos: Jan-26


what model will we start with here?


VN-Cambodia and TH-Laos will start with local SIP model. SIP team is
supporting VN and TH PMO to prep launch, currently on track.


we think that we can open BN to more SEA markets other than MY? How will
the logistics flow work?


Yes we can enable ID/VN/TH/PH exports to BN in Q1-Q2 26, by enabling
fulfillment of their local SIP/Direct shops on MY platform to BN.


Logistics wise, there are 2 options available for each lane: 1) direct
flight into BN; 2) transit from MY/SG into BN

   -

   ID-BN/VN-BN: direct flight option preferred due to lower cost and
   shorter lead time vs transit option
   -

   TH-BN/PH-BN: pending vendor data; likely also direct flight option better


Next step to obtain actual vendor quotations to finalize logistics
solutions; and estimate top

Hi Ian and all,



Please find SIP weekly update attached for review.



Addressing follow-up questions on AME launch from last weekly below
(detailed updates in slides)



   - *Brunei - Aligned with MY AME Launch PMO*


   - *[P0] CNSIP / CN Direct: Jan-26*
      - *[P1] Local SIP / Local Direct: Feb-26*
      - *[P1] KRJP Direct: Feb-26 tentative; MY PMO + MYCB + KRJP team to
      finalize plan*



*For local sip/direct, i thought it was going to be Jan'26?*



Jan’26 P0 segments are 1) MY local shops; 2) CNCB MP shops; 3) CNSIP shops
on MY platform to enable their fulfillment from MY/CN to BN.



For IDBN SIP and IDBN Direct, we target to be ready together with P0
segments by end-Jan, if not latest by 15 Feb to be ready for Raya ramp-up.
Key launch prep actions include logistics solution and channel setup;
global seller center + global wallet testing (for direct); HQ legal
review/modification of TOS.



Pls refer to slide 16 for overall timeline.





*o   **Cambodia & Laos - Aligned with VN/TH AME Launch PMO*

*§  **[P0] VN-Cambodia / TH-Laos: Jan-26*



*what model will we start with here?*



VN-Cambodia and TH-Laos will start with local SIP model. SIP team is
supporting VN and TH PMO to prep launch, currently on track.



*we think that we can open BN to more SEA markets other than MY?

How will
the logistics flow work?*



Yes we can enable ID/VN/TH/PH exports to BN in Q1-Q2 26, by enabling
fulfillment of their local SIP/Direct shops on MY platform to BN.



Logistics wise, there are 2 options available for each lane: 1) direct
flight into BN; 2) transit from MY/SG into BN

   - ID-BN/VN-BN: direct flight option preferred due to lower cost and
   shorter lead time vs transit option
   - TH-BN/PH-BN: pending vendor data; likely also direct flight option
   better



Next step to obtain actual vendor quotations to finalize logistics
solutions; and estimate topline potential based on actual shipping
cost/time to decide order of launch.



Pls refer to slide 17 for detailed logistics options.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 12 – 14 Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>

   -

   How are we relooking Ops policy for other markets?


For SG, SIP implemented 3 actions

#1 Lower price ceiling on category level

#2 Tighten onboarding criteria to admit only proven sellers

#3 Offboard high NFR sellers


For other markets, #1 and #2 are done; #3 ETA 18 Dec after completing biz
impact analysis of using the same threshold in SG to other markets.



Slide 20 Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>

   -

   For JST, why is the # of mature SKUs and SKU efficiency going down?


# mature SKUs -23k Nov vs Oct, due to
+28k new SKUs from mature sellers
-28k SKUs from CO/CL closure

-23k SKUs from non-performing SKU clean up + seller unlist


[image: image.png]

SKU efficiency dropped vs Oct due to

Marketing budget cut in Nov for mature sellers. Mature sellers’P&L
   +$761k Mom (slide 22 below)

Newer SKUs added in Nov diluting the overall SKU efficiency

[image: image.png]

   -

   In general, there are probably too little new SKUs for JST?


Yes. In terms of SKU# per seller, we have about 3k SKU/seller, and when
benchmarked against traditional sources of acquisition such as Lever 1 of
~0.2k/ seller, we are higher.


However, in an absolute SKU # sense, we are working to increase this
through increasing our seller onboarding speed, which will also be
addressed in the question below. The reason for the slow growth is after
our marketing cuts in November, mature sellers experienced fluctuations in
their topline and many new sellers did not generate orders, and this
sparked concerns in the JST sales team to further push more sellers. In the
meantime, we are discussing with JST on the feasibility of an incentive
program where we give them a referral fee for each seller they successfully
refer to us.



   -

   Can we also track seller numbers? How has this grown over time?


We have 302 JST/WDT sellers in total till date, which grew at an average
pace of ~50 sellers per month over the past 5 months since we started the
program.

Currently SIP does not use seller vouchers. This is because SIP uses one
dummy buyer account to “purchase” from an export market shop on behalf of
multiple import market-buyers. By platform policy the dummy buyer account
can only collect 1 seller voucher per shop. We explored the possibility of
collecting 1 seller voucher per shop per end-buyer, but rejected by HQ
legal due to compliance concerns, and ID/VN biz team due to PR/seller
sentiment concerns (e.g., they will see SIP as one buyer

*Agree. We have clarified legal concerns. Legal guidance is that the
eligibility of seller voucher collection should be based on end-buyer
profile and not SIP decision; and the voucher amount needs to be passed to
end-buyer real-time upon collection; this is to legally protect SIP as a
purchase agent for end-buyers, instead of making purchase decisions.
However it conflicts with the current 2-order system design.*

*With above constraints, we are in progress of exploring short-term
workarounds and long-term product solutions with HQ legal and CB BPM. Will
update progress in next weekly update.*



Let’s solve for this. Seller vouchers are meant for end buyers, and SIP is
just a channel we use to allow end buyers to be able to buy. We should be
able to find a workaround for legal concern.



And ID/VN should not have this concern as well.



*For Swarm Document*



Slide 2

   - Can split out OF from here when we look at active sellers and SKUs?

I
   am not sure they will be a big contributor to the business moving forward.
   How does their SKU efficiency compare with JST?

   has been overall slow



Slide 4

   can start investing themselves?

- We will need to set targets for JST and WDT

like Zalora.

Hi Ian,



Slide 20

   - What is the latest on KR SIP?

Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com> Ren Jiahui
   <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>



KRSIP is on track. System requirements have been signed off by KRCB/Reg SIP
team and reviewed with PM.



System readiness target Mar; business prep starts mid-Feb. No major
blocker/risk identified at this stage.





I was referring to pitching status or at least getting a sense check of
seller willingness on this



Do not wish for a situation where we build a solution where the adoption is
low



   - How do we intend to do grow NSS of CB sellers?

Are we referring to CN
   export or Local market export?

Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com> Wei
   Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>



We are referring to local market export (not CN export). This builds on our
structural cost advantage from SLS+SPX network scale; allowing us to offer
lower B2B and B2C rates than market alternatives.



We will start with ID/MY local SPX NSS customer base (~1.2k / 1.3k),
working with NSS team to identify their CB logistics needs and co-build
end-to-end solutions. Both ID/MY SPX teams are onboarded.



Additionally Zalora expressed interest in exploring NSS across its
operating lanes, particularly MY-PH; this is incremental to our core plan.
We are working with RBP team to identify more potential strategic customers
like Zalora.





Slide 36 Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>

   - TH seems very small. Should be much bigger especially on
   Fashion/Beauty front?

In general think we have too little TH sellers on
   direct right now?

Agree. TH is currently under-scaled on Direct, especially in Fashion/Beauty.



This was due to seller acquisition capacity constraints, which previously
limited pitching to ~100 sellers before mid-Dec. The constraint is now
removed with mass pitching and upcoming RM pitching from Jan

o   Mass pitching: started in mid-Dec to ~260k eligible sellers. MTD 1,033
sellers opened direct shops, 947 active selling (vs 69 active sellers in
Nov). Next to continue the weekly pitching cadence.

o   1:1 pitching: planned in Jan-Mar, targeting ~800 priority sellers
chosen by import markets (Mall/brands across SG/MY/PH top 5 categories
including Fashion and Beauty). Target ~60% success rate owned by local RM
(part of local RM KPI).



With both mass and RM pitching active, we expect TH seller growth to
improve meaningfully over next 4-8 weeks, and catch up to peer export
markets by end Q1’26.

Slide 38 Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com> Cuiwei Sun
<cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>

   - How can we grow seller acquisition even faster?

What are the blockers
   right now?

Current status: all eligible sellers have been pitched at least once via
mass or RM pitching. At this stage, the only way to materially accelerate
acquisition is to increase re-pitching success rate (currently ~5%; target
10-15%).



Blockers

o   Primary blocker: local RM incentive/capacity. Direct acquisition is not
part of local RM KPI (other than TH), successful pitching is not rewarded;

o   Secondary blockers: Seller incentives (e.g., low ads/rebate ROI vs
local shop), RM/CS capacity to support onboarding/operations; seller
education capacity - these limit scale-up but not on the critical path to
the faster acquisition.



Solutions

o   Short-term unblocks (Jan-Feb):

§  Reduce RM capacity constraint with 1) focus on Mall and brand sellers;
2) automate RM pitching workflow

§  Revamp seller incentives for Mall/managed sellers to close Direct ROI
gaps with local shops

o   Longer-term unblocks (By Q2-26): strengthen RM incentives 1) formalize
RM KPIs/incentives for Direct with support from export market country
managers and BD HODs; 2) work with HQ finance to enable export market to
earn a Direct service fee to expand RM and export team capacity.



Target to double Mall/managed seller pitching success rate across markets
by end-Mar, and reach ~200k active sellers before 9.9. (2x current, 10% of
local SIP sellers), 4 months ahead of original Dec’26 timeline target.



Slide 39 Ailin Xie <ailin.xie@shopee.com> Huang Wan-Jung
<wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>

   - How about seller vouchers?

Seller vouchers are currently under-utilized on Direct. Voucher ADO
contribution is significantly lower than import market CNCB average. MYSG
is the only exception (summary table below).



*Metric*

*Direct Performance*

This is primarily a seller adoption and incentive design issue, not a
system blocker. MYSG high voucher adoption due to upsized co-share 50-50
voucher incentive scheme covering both BAU and spike days

o   BAU Co-share: a) 18% off, MS $10, Cap $20 b) New Sellers: 12%, MS $5,
Cap $5

o   Spike day Co-share a) 20% Discount Min Spend up to $50 Capped at $50 b)
50% Discount Min Spend $1 Capped at $3



Next steps: 1) work with local CB teams to replicate MYSG best practice to
other top lanes; 2) develop system solutions to default replication of
seller vouchers from local shop to Direct shop on an opt-out basis.



Right now we allow them to port over SKUs from local shop to export shop?
Should we also get them to port over their seller voucher?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

This is hard to understand. How many SMT 1-3 sellers have we pitched to?
What is the contribution to platform, what is the success rate?

We have not kicked off the seller pitching yet. Above mentioned is a seller
sentiment survey to collect interests/concerns about the SIP model. The 55
sellers are all SMT2 and SMT3 brand sellers (18% KRCB ADO coverage,
excluding SMT2/3 resellers). Their opinions are representative of managed
sellers overall. We will bake their feedback into seller
pitching/acquisition materials to address their concerns early.

KRCB and SIP team are jointly working on a pitching plan based on the
seller segments, will be ready for review by 23 Jan (SIP Jan monthly
meeting). Actual pitching will start from mid-Feb to catch the system
launch in Mar.

Agree to enhance system features to enable Direct sellers to replicate
local shop vouchers to Direct shops. We are working with CB BPM to finalize
a P1 product feature request by mid Jan.

We will also track the seller voucher adoption rate and work on a seller
communication/incentive plan with each market. We will prioritize the mall
and managed seller group who have a voucher gap between local market and
export market first.



When will have updates regarding this?


We are finalizing feature design and impact estimation. CB BPM will email a
P0 request for your approval by this Friday (16 Jan), and proceed to RBX
approval in jan cycle. Target to launch the feature by Q1 with top
priority. Estimated dev effort is 1.5-2 months. CB BPM will closely monitor
the progress to mitigate any timeline risks.

*Subject:* Re: [Email update] Dec-25 SIP monthly meeting



Hi Ian,



*This is hard to understand. How many SMT 1-3 sellers have we pitched to?
What is the contribution to platform, what is the success rate?

*

We have not kicked off the seller pitching yet. Above mentioned is a seller
sentiment survey to collect interests/concerns about the SIP model. The 55
sellers are all SMT2 and SMT3 brand sellers (18% KRCB ADO coverage,
excluding SMT2/3 resellers). Their opinions are representative of managed
sellers overall. We will bake their feedback into seller
pitching/acquisition materials to address their concerns early.

KRCB and SIP team are jointly working on a pitching plan based on the
seller segments, will be ready for review by 23 Jan (SIP Jan monthly
meeting). Actual pitching will start from mid-Feb to catch the system
launch in Mar.



Agree to enhance system features to enable Direct sellers to replicate
local shop vouchers to Direct shops. We are working with CB BPM to finalize
a P1 product feature request by mid Jan.

We will also track the seller voucher adoption rate and work on a seller
communication/incentive plan with each market. We will prioritize the mall
and managed seller group who have a voucher gap between local market and
export market first.



*When will have updates regarding this?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,
please find the answers to your questions regarding the 2026 LFF target.

*Follow Up Question 1: Sharing recap of 2026 LFF bottom up projection
calculation methodology*

A: The calculation logic for 2026 LFF target projection is as follows:
LFF 2026 ADO Projection = Existing LFF SKU ADO + New LFF SKU ADO
New LFF SKU ADO = Total Addressable New LFF SKUs * Historical SLS to LFF
CVR% * Target SKU Efficiency









*Note: *Existing SKU ADO = Existing SKU# * Target SKU efficiency*New SKU
ADO = New SKU# * Target SKU efficiency*New SKU# = Addressable SKU# *
Conversion %*SKU# is model level*Existing SKU = SKUs inbounded before ‘26
Jan 1*New SKU = SKUs inbounding starting ‘26 Jan 1*Addressable SKU# = # of
managed brands’ SLS SKU with  ADI>=0.5*Existing SKU# is projected based on
historical SKU# depreciation trends and new SKU# is projected based on
target conversion % from the addressable SKU pool, which is managed brands’
SLS SKUs with ADI ≥ 0.5. Each existing and new target ADO is projected by
multiplying the respective target SKU# by the corresponding target SKU
efficiency.*

*Follow Up Question 2: Review 2026 TH LFF target (consider total LFF
eligible shops and SKUs based on current ADIS, only including managed brand
sellers), new SLS to LFF SKU CVR% projection given TH SLS tax hike
(benchmarking SLS to LFF SKU CVR change when SLS tax was implemented in
other markets)*

[image: image.png]
A: We adjusted 2026 Dec TH LFF target ADO from 2,745 to 3,442 (TH LFF%
increased by 9%p, increasing 2026 total LFF% by 1%p). The order projection
increase is expected to come from new LFF SKUs, as we increased the target
SLS SKU conversion rate from 16% to 32%, benchmarking MY’s SLS SKU
conversion rate growth at 2x after the '24 LVG tax hike from SLS.

*Follow Up Question 3: Check TH 2026 FBS space capacity and whether TH
needs to lower down ADIS (also check whether there is meaningful ADO if we
do lower down ADIS)*

A: TH 2026 FB

19bb716073a0d113.deck_filename: [For Phyllis Approval] [SLS CNTW] [Seaway LM] 7-11 Seaway LM Quotation Update starting from Jan. 2026.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

I am writing on behalf of the SLS team to seek your approval for the *following
TW last-mile 3PL arrangements*, effective from 1 January 2026:
*1) 7-11 Seaway – New contract*, with a proposed fee of *NTD 38 per parcel
(previously NTD 28)*, citing channel volume decline and internal cost
increases.
*2) OK Mart – Contract addendum*, with a proposed fee increase from *NTD 15
to NTD 20 per parcel*, citing rising internal operating costs. For context,
OK Mart has not implemented any fee increases over the past three years.

TW Legal has requested that the *final approval will need to be given by
Chris,* since the contract values exceed $100k USD.

For your reference, we have included the following additional information:
*1) Rate comparison with other LM vendors*
[image: image.png]

*2) P&L impact to SLS*
Their combined channel mix is 16.7%, with CNTW overall PPO projected to
decrease* 0.017 USD (2.6%)*
Detailed cost impact and rate summary by channel:
[image: image.png]

I have also attached the prior approvals from Shuning and Phyllis for your
reference.

Please let us know if you require any further information. Otherwise, we
kindly seek your approval to proceed with the contract execution. Thank you.

Best regards,
-- 
*Yu Kai Shen, 余凱勝*
Shopee | Cross Border | Taiwan CB
Mobile: +65 9622 4333
Address: 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

content.question:
+ @Robert Liu <robert.liu@shopee.com>



Have we checked with our TW team to see if the rates that we have gotten is
fair?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

We have aligned with both the CNCB SLS and TW teams and believe the
proposed rates are reasonable for the following reasons：
*1) OK Mart*
- They have* not implemented any fee increases over the past three years*
and continues to *offer the lowest per-parcel rate* among all LM vendors

*2) 7-Eleven Seaway*
- 7-Eleven Seaway previously provided significant discounts driven by
higher volumes. However, due to volume decline, they are no longer able to
sustain these discounted rates.
-  Despite the increase to NTD 38 per parcel, the rate remains *lower
than 7-Eleven Airway and Local shipping*, both priced at NTD 42.5 per parcel

To mitigate the impact of these fee increases, we have implemented the
*following
3 initiatives to shift more volume to the SPX sea-shipping channel:*
1) Starting from 1 Jan 2026, 7-Eleven sea shipping is updated to 299 ntd
min spend free shipping (previously no min spend), with plans to remove
free shipping from March
2) SLS has proactively enabled the SPX sea-shipping channel for all items
that meet channel eligibility requirements
3) The display priority of shipping channels has been adjusted on both the
buyer-side filter and checkout page to increase the likelihood of SPX
sea-shipping being selected

Thank you, please let us know if you have any further questions.

Regards,
Kai Shen

content.answer:
For your reference, 7-11 also increased the shipping fee for TW local
orders using the same reasons - 7-11's order volume dropped in 2025.

We are pushing more orders from 7-11 to SPX this year by offering unlimited
SPX free shipping for all sellers, and cutting the FSV for 7-11.
Also, SPX will expand more locker stores this year (from ~2900 last Dec to
4100 in Dec'26) to grow our capactiy for both local and CB orders.

content.answer:
Hi Ian

To answer your question

*I thought we were spending -1 on new sellers? Seems like it has increased
to -2.2 - -2.4 now? What changed?*

Sorry for the confusion, swarm new seller UE was kept at -2 to -3 from Nov
to Jan (*-$2.24)*. Instead our program P&L (seller+sls+MP PC2) is -1.x (
*-$1.42* for Jan)


Also to address your question on swarm UE vs MP new sellers*: are we
investing more or less than new MP sellers? and what's our long term plan?*

*Conclusion*: we are investing *2.08* usd/order *more* than CNCB MP mainly
due to


   - a) SLS discount to swarm as swarm order weight is heavier, which we
   agreed to enjoy the heavy rate table
   - b) subsidy in seller P&L to drive new sku growth.

*In the long run by end of year, seller P&L needs to breakeven as a whole*
+ SLS discount will stay for those heavy weight items/shops.


*For details, please refer to table below:*
[image: image.png]

Thanks

wow, i thought you told me before new seller for MP was -0.7? now it is

trends are evident:

#2: After three incubation months, UE has recovered to around *-1.2*
usd/order. We are working towards to achieve positive P&L on mature Shops
through seller co-sponsorship and optimised investment by *Apr'26*
#3: As the seller portfolio matures, overall (new+old skus) UE is projected
to *break even* , new shops UE is projected to improved to *-1.35 **by
Dec’26*

Thanks

   - [BR] +7% vs last submission, to reflect latest CB penetration, FY26 at
   5.8% (last cycle CB penetration proj. 5.3% was benchmark Dec’25, when CB
   penetration normally low during holiday seasons)
   - [TH] -3% vs last submission, to refresh TH import tax impact based on
   actual performance, with CNCB 3P SLS seeing ~22% order drop vs Jan H1 BAU
   days (last cycle factored in ~16% drop)

#2 CB P&L:

   -

   -

      [ALL]: KRJP Commission Fee increase from Feb’26 and Compulsory Ads
      from Mar’26
      -

      [VN]: Non-ads takerate +0.7p.p mainly from 3PF higher Platform
      Support Fee/GMV % when ABS drop
      -

   SLS: FY26 P&L at $296mil, vs. last submission +10mil. Gaps mainly driven
   by topline changes:
   -

      [PH]: +2.4m from higher 3P SLS orders (+2.0m) from LFF downward
      revision (-2% ADO) and UE improvement (+1.3m) mainly from lower LH and CC
      vendor price down
      -

      [MY]: +3.0m mainly from higher 3P SLS orders (+4.2%) from 3PF
      downward revision (ADO -6%) offset by LH cost increase
(+0.06/kg, -$0.9mil)

      quotation vs last cycle and FX gain (+1.5m), net off by Jan tax
loss due to
      import duty under collection (-3m). *Note that despite we charge
      import duty tax with a $0.38/pc tax surplus, this can not be
booked in SLS
      P&L per Sea Fin/tax guidance

Hi Ian


Pls find reply as follows


From Ian: For KR, is there any chance we can get a discounted rate similar
to CNCB? How do we go about doing so? We probably need to move more TH
sellers to LFF, the impact is quite big.

think your suggestion to move to LFF is more recommended rather than giving
a discounted tax charge, reasons being

   -

   KR is air channel which CC vendors needs to pay to customs that align
   with official announced tax rate, so if to keep the discounted tax rate,
   the tax under collection loss needs to be funded by Shopee (which is the
   Jan 19-Feb 8th case)
   -

   However, the investment is *not so rewarding*, we need to spend *135k
   usd *monthly more vs we charge full tax, but very small ADO/ADGMV uplift
   (see table 1 yellow highlighted)

topline performances using actual change results)

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Remove rest to answer your question.

I see, we cannot do it how CNCB does it?

with TH customs, so,


   - from P&L wise, we will have* tax over collection if we charge 100% tax
   revenue but pay 70% tax cost to customs,* and by right, we shouldn't
   "overcharge" tax and spend the tax gain
   - from counter competitor wise, which is more importantly, TT is
   charging sellers by discounted rate, to avoid seller confusion + lose
   NPC/SOB, need to go with discount rate

Best,

*Faye Yan*

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 3 Engielyn Toh <engielyn.toheq@shopee.com>

   -

   Why is seller standalone for CNSIP negative?
   -

      Seller standalone for CNSIP is now breakeven, latest projection +$8k
      to +$67k
      -

      However it is below the P&L target (+$122k) due to one-time system
      issue when setting up 2% comms rebate. The issue is resolved. Expected
      CNSIP seller P&L to be positive and hit target from Feb onwards.
      -

   Swarm is going to lose much more than expected?
   -

      Yes, Jan will miss target, mainly due to VN order higher than
      expected with VN platform growth, but VN UE is lower than other markets
      -

      We’ve already raised prices twice in Jan, which improved UE but not
      enough to fully close the gap.
      -

      Feb plan is clear: further price increases to bring UE to -$0.99
      (+$0.33 MoM) and seller P&L to -$133k (+$77k MoM).


Slide 4 Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>

   -

   Without Flash, we still have SPX?
   -

      Yes, SPX is the primary last-mile provider to SIP.
      -

      However, SPX coverage in East Malaysia is currently partial; ~200 SIP
      orders in EM are not yet serviceable by SPX (or BEST) following the Flash
      switch.
      -

      The impact is small and contained; we are in progress of confirming
      with MY PIC on the timeline to enable SPX coverage for the remaining
      high-ADO areas in EM.


Slide 7 Engielyn Toh <engielyn.toheq@shopee.com>

   -

   P&L different than Slide 3? Why P&L drop so much MOM? Seller P&L should
   be positive for CNSIP regardless of target setting?
   -

      Both Slide 3 and this page are consistent - seller PnL breakeven,
      -$122k below target, -$94k MoM.
      -

      The gap vs target and MoM drop are not business deterioration;
      instead both are driven by a one-time system setup issue when
applying the

*Slide 3 **Engielyn Toh* <engielyn.toheq@shopee.com>

   - *Why is seller standalone for CNSIP negative?*


   - Seller standalone for CNSIP is now breakeven, latest projection +$8k
      to +$67k
      - However it is below the P&L target (+$122k) due to one-time system
      issue when setting up 2% comms rebate. The issue is resolved. Expected
      CNSIP seller P&L to be positive and hit target from Feb onwards.


   - *Swarm is going to lose much more than expected?*

      with VN platform growth, but VN UE is lower than other markets
      - We’ve already raised prices twice in Jan, which improved UE but not
      enough to fully close the gap.
      - Feb plan is clear: further price increases to bring UE to -$0.99
      (+$0.33 MoM) and seller P&L to -$133k (+$77k MoM).





UE here is seller or program?

When is it ready?*


   - KRSIP system request under PRD, on track to be ready by end Mar’26
      - Business rollout: Seller pitching to start from mid-Mar, start to
      sell from early Apr


   - *When will we pass chat to sellers?*


   - *Direct*: currently ID export shop chats are handled by SIP agents; in
      progress of returning chats to sellers and complete by 2 Mar. All other
      lanes chats are handled by sellers already.
      - *SIP*: system request currently under PM’s feasibility study;
      target end Jun’26 live; business rollout from Jul’26 starting
from AME lanes

      by mid-Feb; target to go live by *Apr/May*
      - *SIP*: Biz requirement confirmed, pending HQ legal assessment.
      Target to go live by *end-Q2*



We should be pushing for Seller vouchers for SIP first?

impact?

content.question: *How are we growing ID and VN direct?

Who is owning this piece?*

   - *Ownership*: ID and VN Direct growth is owned by the *local ID and VN
   export teams*, who commit export seller and topline targets to their
   country managers respectively. They are the primary KPI and business owners
   for export business, accountable for delivery lane by lane, and mobilizing
   local RM resources and RM KPI.
   - *Role of the Regional Direct team (SIP team)*: The Regional Direct
   team is the regional program owner, responsible for program design, core
   enablers, and scalability. The team focuses on building up system and
   commercial foundations on a regional scale, and removing structural
   constraints so local teams can grow faster, more predictably, and at scale.
   - *How ID/VN Direct growth is being driven*
      - *Seller & SKU acquisition*: Local export teams lead acquisition and
      repitching (Mall/managed sellers, import-market wishlists such as
      Raya/CNY), with incentives aligned to import market priorities.
      - *SKU supply & efficiency*: Regional Direct team continuously drives
      system and ops improvements (SKU migration, global wallet, seller voucher
      resync) to expand usable supply and improve seller ROI.
      - *Capacity unlock*: To address limited local export RM capacity,
      Regional Direct team has built automation tools (RM bots,
acquisition bots)
      and is piloting AI-led seller support (Digital RM) to structurally scale
      export RM capacity, starting from ID.
   - *Operating model:* Business targets are jointly set by local ID/VN
   teams and the Regional Direct team, aligned with import market CB teams in
   each lane based on import market strategic priority. The targets are
   reviewed with clear ownership and follow-up actions on a regional level
   once monthly.





What are the targets here for the year?

*We should be pushing for Seller vouchers for SIP first?

That has higher
impact?*

   - Yes, aligned voucher resync for SIP has higher impact; but also
   significantly higher complexity compared to Direct, so it requires more
   time to sort-out.
   - Key constraints we are optimizing in our system design process
now: 1) *meet
   legal requirements* to ensure voucher collection is seen as an
   individual buyer decision/action instead of SIP; 2) *mitigate SIP P&L
   risk* when voucher cost allocation to the end seller is
unsuccessful; 3) *reduce
   negative seller sentiment* if they are unwilling to use local vouchers
   for overseas orders.
   - Direct model is more straightforward as Direct sellers fully control
   voucher mechanics and P&L for their overseas shops. This allows us to move
   Direct into the pipeline faster with lower execution risk.
   - Therefore, we moved forward with Direct, while continuing SIP design
   and HQ legal alignment in parallel, targeting design readiness before CNY.

seller repitching

o   Global wallet rollout (Mar–Apr) to simplify ads investment

o   Seller voucher sync (Apr–May) to replicate local vouchers for Direct
shops

o   Explore digital RM bot to support Direct seller growth

*Can we always show both import and export views in future?*

   - Yes. Will include both import and export views in all future reports
   on direct selling.



*How are we growing ID and VN direct?

Who is owning this piece?*

   - *Ownership*: ID and VN Direct growth is owned by the *local ID and VN
   export teams*, who commit export seller and topline targets to their
   country managers respectively. They are the primary KPI and business owners
   for export business, accountable for delivery lane by lane, and mobilizing
   local RM resources and RM KPI.
   - *Role of the Regional Direct team (SIP team)*: The Regional Direct
   team is the regional program owner, responsible for program design, core
   enablers, and scalability. The team focuses on building up system and
   commercial foundations on a regional scale, and removing structural
   constraints so local teams can grow faster, more predictably, and at scale.
   - *How ID/VN Direct growth is being driven*


   - *Seller & SKU acquisition*: Local export teams lead acquisition and
      repitching (Mall/managed sellers, import-market wishlists such as
      Raya/CNY), with incentives aligned to import market priorities.
      - *SKU supply & efficiency*: Regional Direct team continuously drives
      system and ops improvements (SKU migration, global wallet, seller voucher
      resync) to expand usable supply and improve seller ROI.
      - *Capacity unlock*: To address limited local export RM capacity,
      Regional Direct team has built automation tools (RM bots,
acquisition bots)
      and is piloting AI-led seller support (Digital RM) to structurally scale
      export RM capacity, starting from ID.


   - *Operating model:* Business targets are jointly set by local ID/VN
   teams and the Regional Direct team, aligned with import market CB teams in
   each lane based on import market strategic priority. The targets are
   reviewed with clear ownership and follow-up actions on a regional level
   once monthly.





*Slide 27*

·  *SIP is not in VN right now?
Do we know why?

Wei Qiang
<wei.qiang@shopee.com>*

o   Local SIP was into VN but closed in 2022 H2 due to low ADO potential
~600 with high RR/COD/logistics cost.

o   We’re now preparing a re-entry with lower logistics cost ($1 less vs
2022), differentiated assortment from TH/MY, and TH GR needs, starting with
TH→VN.

*What is the sense of ADO impact here?* *We do not think ID/VN would make
sense?*

   - SIP plans to re-enter Vietnam starting March with assortments from

   ID/MY/PH–VN launches planned for April-May 26 following the TH–VN rollout.
   - Total ADO projection is* ~4.1k by Dec’26*.

Hi Ian and Jianghong,

following key topics

   - CB Performance update on Jan’26 Review & Feb’26 Projections
   - Key Initiatives Updates :
      - Commission Waiving 2026

Hi Ian and Jianghong,

To summarize our conclusion from the meeting regarding the push direction
for Swarm. Let us know if further to clarify.

*Swarm serves the purpose of bringing in new assortments to our platform,
hence,*

   -

   For Unique SKUs/Sellers, shall be our main focus to push with following
   two guidelines
   -

      Build up role models with incubation and mature push plans
      -

      Clear path on pushing effectiveness (e.g. CR uplift) and long term
      profitability possibility
      -

   For SKUs that are inevitably brought to the platform that are existing
   Shopee’s SKUs, we shall target with at least P&L breakeven from the very
   beginning and earn money to sponsor the unique sellers/SKUs in 3 months
   -

   Specifically for OF, we will target to breakeven from Mar'26 and run
   organically


*With this in mind,*

   -

   From SIP P&L team, pricing accurately from the time when SKUs in, based
   on unique SKUs pool and mature stage to allocate marketing budget
   -

   From SIP Mkt team, 1) selectively choose role model and define push plan

identify BD focus with uniqueness and demand
   -

   From CN BD team, acquire targeted sellers by 1) built up role model 2)
   BD focus of SIP mkt team provided pattern


*Next Step:*

   -

   SIP Swarm team to adjust operations plan based on above alignment
   -

   SIP Swarm team to re-budget for upcoming month performance and update
   progress in weekly report


Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

   -

      Seller MoM growth slowdown is expected and reflected in FY26 plan.
      -

         We completed R1 mass pitching and have shifted to 1:1 repitching
         of higher-quality Mall/managed sellers, which reduces volume
but improves
         seller quality.
         -

      *Temporary capacity factors also impacted active sellers coun*t, but
      they are seasonal and won’t change FY26 growth trajectory
      -

         The ID team prioritized Raya push and paused mass pitching.
         -

         Some VN sellers went on Tet holiday and shifted shops to HM.
         -

      SKU count drop in ID and VN is not expected and mainly driven by 2
      reasons
      -

         VN sellers shifted shops to HM (~100k SKU drop)
         -

         VN/ID SKU cleanup by import markets based on import market listing
         limit policy (~400k SKU drop) - this is a known BAU regional
ops cleanup;
         didn’t surface earlier as the fast growth of SKUs in the
previous months

to explore a

content.question: are we happy with the progress of program pitching?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

In short, yes we saw
1) High seller adoption of the penetration program (>90%)
2) Meaningful improvements to FBS ADO% to Shop ADO (34.7%, +3pp MoM, higher
vs Nov/Dec '25) and are on track to our target.
However, there is room to optimize further based on feedback we have
collected from sellers, and we will continue to monitor seller adoption at
the seller level closely.

More details below on what we will do differently:
- Program Mechanics: We currently give 1 quarter for sellers to hit the
target FBS ADO%, and as long as they hit this target, they will enjoy
incentive for the rest of the quarter if they maintain above target.
- Issue: For the sellers who did not adopt the program/fail the targets,
they think the targets we set are stretched and they only get rewarded at
the end of the quarter for hitting the target penetration, effectively
cutting down the duration they enjoy the incentive should they only hit the
target at the end of the quarter.
- Next steps: To improve adoption for these sellers, we will break down
quarterly targets to monthly targets so that sellers who need time to
prepare new SKU inventory are incentivised to grow as well. This is aligned
with CN team and we will monitor the improvement in adoption/effectiveness
of this program tweak.

on #3, how will we manage tracking moving forward, and how will we reward
(good peerformers)/punish (poor performers)?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see below for our response, feel free to let me know if you have any
questions.

Q1. on #3, how will we manage tracking moving forward, and how will we
reward (good performers)/punish (poor performers)?

Tracking>> current practice lacks tracking except for Quarter end sharing
to SMT leads/people team for incentive payout. To better drive action, we
are working on: 1. Auto-update tracker building; 2. Biweekly RR review for
timely de-bottle neck; 3. Monthly scoring to RMs. Target to complete in
line with Q2 KPI roll-out.

Reward scheme>> current practice is through quarterly bonus based on team
KPI performance tiering (e.g. if SMT1 achieved tier 1 RR, all RMs under
SMT1 will receive tier 1 bonus). We are working with people team to
benchmark with local/CN practices focusing on 1. incentive based on team or
individual level performance; 2. Whether year-end bonus should tie with
quarterly KPI performance. Will get back to you with a proposal before Q2
KPI roll-out.

Thanks



On #1, do we need so long to get to the bottom of the situation?
Where did we land on the insurance topic for JP sellers?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see below for our responses, feel free to let us know if you have
any questions.

On #1, do we need so long to get to the bottom of the situation?



We will send you a detailed update by EOW. Preliminary findings are:


Commission TR

G2N ratio contributes to majority of the gap, followed by delta in market
and category mix - we are working with reg CB FP&A on alignment of #s and
forward-looking improvement areas.


Ads TR

Key reason for missing ads TR is because MS target is set on absolute spend
basis and not on TR basis. Even though the team hits their ads spend target
(103% RR), it did not scale fast enough to match the topline which grew
faster than plan (123% RR).

Next steps we will fix the performance tracking to monitor both spend and
TR and focus on scaling up ads spending in SMT1 sellers to recover the take
rate; March catchup plans to be shared later.

Where did we land on the insurance topic for JP sellers?


Currently SLS collects insurance fee(1.75% of COGs excl. VAT for all JP
orders) from sellers upfront and any compensation is paid out from the same
bucket and only when additional net cost incurs it is booked under SLS
other cost. Sellers can get compensation if an order is returned/refund
requested for reasons such as change of mind, wrong item etc. We have
confirmed with monee that there is no issue with expediting the payment and
there is also no risk of double payments via system.


We will therefore proceed with expedited 50% compensation payout to all
affected JP sellers as discussed and have already communicated this to
sellers.


The first batch will be processed for affected orders between Jan 27 to Feb
25:

   -

   Payouts scheduled between Mar W4 and Apr W1
   -

   Compensation amount estimated at USD 234k


The second batch is planned to be processed for affected orders between Feb
26 to Mar 11.


In the meantime we are collating seller sentiment and feedb

G2N ratio contributes to majority of the gap, followed by delta in market

*Ads TR*

Key reason for missing ads TR is because MS target is set on absolute spend
basis and not on TR basis. Even though the team hits their ads spend target
(103% RR), it did not scale fast enough to match the topline which grew
faster than plan (123% RR).

Next steps we will fix the performance tracking to monitor both spend and
TR and focus on scaling up ads spending in SMT1 sellers to recover the take
rate; March catchup plans to be shared later.

Hi Ian,

Please see our responses below; feel free to let me know if you have any
questions.

thanks, what are next steps then?

Next steps are twofold:
1. Market / site expansion
- Seller-level targets have already been identified. The next step is to
move beyond target identification and assess which initiatives can actually
drive progress.
- We will preparea more detailed approach through seller calls and plan to
start reaching out to sellers toward the end of this week to gather
additional insights.
- Based on these insights, we will formulate a more concrete action plan in
early April (After immersion trip - Mar 23–27).
2. SKU migration
- We are currently aligning on this with the project team.
- SKU migration is included in the OKR, so track target setting and RR
progress will closely be monitored.

We've also completed deep-dive on TR gap and please see below for details.

For Feb we see a TR gap of 1.9% for KRCB and 0.7% for JPCB respectively,
with KRCB mainly driven by ads take rate gap and JPCB mainly driven by
non-ads take rate gap (commission, service fee, and handling fee).

content.question: why do we think it does not make sense to arrange a trip for TW?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Let me clarify. It is not that we are ruling out a separate TW trip.
Our preference is simply to have the TW team join this trip first, mainly
due to (1) the urgency around counter-CP and (2) cost considerations.

Believe the better sequencing is to first have the TW team join this trip,
and then evaluate a TW-focused seller trip at a later stage, potentially
around the timing of the TW FBS launch.

Best,
Junho

Ian,

We wanted to share a quick update on TW involvement for the upcoming TH/VN
immersion trip.
We checked with the relevant teams on this Tue, and TW will not be able to
join this round. Given the timing, it would be challenging to coordinate
participation and secure sufficient airtime at short notice.
That said, we are still keeping the idea of a separate TW-focused immersion
trip in mind as a possible next step.

In the short term, we are planning an offline seminar for all managed
sellers, where the TW team would join to share the market opportunity in
Taiwan and why this should be a focus area. We are also looking to use this
session to further pitch SIP.
At this stage, we are targeting mid-April to early May, in line with the
SIP launch timing. We will align internally with the TW team on the next
steps.

Best,
Junho Kim

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Thanks for your comments. Please find our responses below in *blue*.

slide 5 - 11 Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>

- who takes ownership of the products once it leaves the seller and brought
out export market?


Seller retains ownership until sale; unsold inventory to be returned to
seller via RTS

This is a consignment model, subject to legal/finance/tax clearance.


- this is probably similar to SCS LFF model? and if we want to run 1 single
shop, we should not have so many "choice" shops running in 1 market?


Yes, SIP LFF follows a consignment model similar to SCS.


However, we propose a separate shop as SIP LFF operates on a different
assortment and sourcing model.

   -

   Assortment/commercial strategy
   -

      Focus on SEA-sourced, unique / higher ABS SKUs
      -

      Requires different pricing, marketing, and budget allocation approach
      -

   Sourcing & operating model
   -

      Direct sourcing from fragmented SIP sellers (no existing
      supply/inventory to leverage)
      -

      Leads to different supply chain setup, cadence, and SOPs
      -

   P&L optimization
   -

      Part of SIP seller P&L
      -

      Enable more targeted budget allocation and ROI optimization/synergy
      -

   GR/external considerations
   -

      Lower GR/PR sensitivity (esp. ID)
      -

      Higher likelihood of ASEAN government / export funding support


- the upside of 3K ado does not feel very high in the grand scheme of
things? and how are we accounting for the p&l of black stock (given that
once it leaves export market), we are probably responsible for it

Yes the ~3k ADO upside is relatively small, driven by 1) limited SIP
addressable pool; 2) expected low (~30%) seller onboarding rate due to
fragmented base and consignment model.

Why we still propose to proceed

   -

   Strategic direction: Local fulfillment is a key platform shift; SIP as a
   seller need

over 3 months
   -

   Acquisition aligned to SKU profile focus: CN MKT team focus acquisition
   on unique and price-competitive SKUs


With this targeted investment approach focusing on quality SKUs, we expect
to have better investment ROI. Next steps to 1) monitor topline and ROI; 2)
automate SKU profiling + investment process for all Swarm sellers/SKUs by
Apr.


Appendix: Investment approach and target UE based on SKU profiles for
role-model building

[image: image.png]


- in general, i am not sure whether we are doing the right thing other than
just increasing price and making the UE better. On whether we are selecting
the right assortment to list and push..


We are not relying on price alone, we are strengthening fundamentals to
ensure scalable, sustainable growth of Swarm business model, including

   -

   P&L discipline: Price at right level to restore healthy UE
   -

   Investment discipline: Focus spend on quality SKUs (unique /
   price-competitive); limit investment/incubation duration to max 3 months
   -

   Seller quality improvement: reinforce seller co-investment with better
   pricing and better SKUs; remove low-quality sellers with ops /
   fulfillment issues
   -

   Targeted assortment strategy: Focus on acquiring quality SKUs that are
   unique / price-competitive


With above measures in place, we have much better control on SKU quality,
investment ROI, and P&L. We now have the right foundation to scale topline
sustainably while ensuring P&L health.



slide 21 -22 Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>

- i cannot reconcile the 2 tables here, can give me in monthly and annual
figures? and maybe we look at the next 3 months first. and what oil prices
are we modelling for here?


Modeling approach

   -

   FSC requested by airlines: $0.12-$0.20/kg, averaging ~$0.16/kg, which
   translates to an $0.08 LH CPO increase
   -

   Monthly impact modeled based on CPO increase

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see our reponses below, feel free to let me know if you have any
questions.

 slide 11

- how is our ado in BR so far? have we considered FBS for BR yet?

   -

   BR Mar MTD ADO is 4,966(P6M CMGR 7%, 7% contri% to KR) and we explored
   BR 3PF option in ‘25 Jul but de-prioritized; 1) have less E2E cost
   competitiveness vs. SLS to sellers(5%p higher E2E cost) and 2) only white
   channel is available which also contributes to higher seller cost(e.g
   customs tax, license registration fee) from 1) not being able to
   under-value 2) license requirement

[image: image.png]


slide 12

- for VN, we are still unable to use local FBS? still have to utilize LFF?

-> yes, currently system dev KRSC <> VN local FBS is in progress(ETA ‘26
Q2-Q3), thus will push for FBS conversion right after. Conversion to FBS
expected to go smoothly as;

   -

   Better cost economics for seller: seller E2E cost lower, especially from
   16% lower WH cost vs. 3PF
   -

   Higher order conversion from more exposure and FBS tag


slide 16

- for #2 >> what is the issue around API integration right now?

   -

   For KR platform to onboard, API integration is essential for efficient
   OMS/WMS. Musinsa wants to check the complexity of API integration with SHP
   and currently waiting to receive their system requirements/specs to review
   with SHP BPM

- for #9 >> how are they doing right now?

   -

   Confirmed 2 brand (out of 100+) onboarding and in the process of
   onboarding more brands
   -

   Shop operation-wise, listing done but they have not started marketing
   yet as they plan to open offline store simultaneously. Will start
   promotions from Apirl with NSP participation


slide 19

- how does the situation in JP look like?

- given that we will have alot of new hires, should we relook our
onboarding process as well?

   -

   We prioritized KR test and will roll-out JP quiz after tailoring

Starting from Mar 15, shortening lead time from parcel scan in to export
   cc start to BAU level (2~3 days vs. currently 3~4 days) via new SOPs with
   3rd party vendors

Starting from Apr 1, negotiate with the export customs clearance agents
   for less frequent manifest inspection and more daily export clearance quota

We can only assess the impact of both (1) and (2) starting after Mar 24
   and mid-Apr if we consider that it is taking 12+ days from order placed to
   buyer delivery and because system tracking is limited from no line haul
   integration with Sagawa, we only know when parcel is scanned in the
   last-mile sorting center

- i remember we were looking at getting another CC vendor, where did we
land on that?

   -

   We invited 12 vendors  to submit their line haul/cc quotations on 3/20,
   aiming to decide on the final vendor by Apr 24, and go live with the new
   vendors Jul 1.
   -

   [RFQ & Go-live Timeline]

[image: image.png]

- did we proactively increase the EDT so that we do not disappoint on buyer
experience?

   -

   Yes, we are continuously increasing EDD/EDT by 10days(from Feb 11th for
   EDD) and will addt’l adjust EDD/EDT after monitoring the BWT this week as
   well

slide 31

- have we paid out the SLS + compensation yet?

   -

   The first round of the seller compensation is expected to be paid out to
   the sellers Apr W1(which is what we announced to sellers)
   -

   We expect that the compensation will be made on a rolling basis for
   orders placed late Jan~late Feb/early Mar, and subsequent compensations
   will be made weekly

- where did we land on getting compensation from vendor?

   -

   We are currently working with the legal team to define the scope of
   potential claims. The areas under review include:
   -

      Seller compensation payouts
      -

      SHP costs incurred, including operational expenses
      -

      Storage fees resulting from delays
      -

      Reputational damage (if applicable)
      -

   Additionally, we have aligned to submit the compensation claim later in
   Q2 to maintain an amicable relationship with Sagawa. This is particularly
   important as (1) we have not yet secured a new line haul vendor, and (2) we
   intend to continue us

[short term] Accelerating sku onboard of the top reseller varient.kr
   (capable of sourcing ~25% unmatched F&B SPUs) with ops support

[short term] Start manage CS F&B resellers (~400 GPs identified) with
   high potential
[mid term] Swarm project for mass SKU upload by end of May

[mid-long term] F&B brand/reseller acquisition, expecting 1 new F&B
   brand to onboard by H1

Hi Shuning,

As aligned with Van,

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

The ~20% total increment is mainly driven by factoring in Shopee’s average
2-month bonus, with the monthly base salary increasing by ~11% (from SGD
5,200 to SGD 5,800), and also to compare with candidate profile (3 years of
experience), we think SGD 5,800 (P50 for Associate level) is reasonable,
and we expect her to ramp up and contribute faster than other fresh
graduates.

let me know if you have any questions, thank you.
*Emily Lin, 林佑容*

waivers for some new 3PF sellers ended in Feb / Mar. This could pose
potential pressure for VN 3PF ADO growth.

In contrast, TTS shows a higher LFF ADO pene% in VN compared with SHP (64%
vs 42%), thus TTS’s robust LFF growth can largely drive the overall CB
topline growth (updated in slides)

underperforming the platform by -0.15x. This is primarily due to quick TTS
CNCB ramp-up (ADG +29% QoQ) by aggressively expanding LFF sellers
specialized in large & bulky categories with higher ABS

   PHCB to gain market share

The category mix remains broadly consistent between PH local and CB, with
nuances in FMCG and EL. (Note: local has slightly higher FMCG presence with
a lean EL, while CB features a more balanced split)

Slide 8 -  +17% below platform growth,  the

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see replies below in blue and updated slides attached, let me know
if any other questions.

Additionally, have replaced SOB with Slide 3 which combines view of CB/CNLS
to show total SOB improvement vs platform, moved CB/CNLS split into
appendix.

Slide 8 (Now Slide 5)

   -

   For CB, can breakdown into SLS and LFF? 17% yoy is not very good

Added in deck, can also see the breakdown in the table below. Table may
trigger comparison questions (e.g., why SLS MP PC2 is negative, why LFF YoY
PC2% fell). A cleaner narrative for ADG: +17% YoY, despite not strong, it’s
straightforward to explain,

   -

   with most of the gap driven by TH/VN downturns (platform +35%/+25% vs.
   CB +16%/0%)
   -

   Also note on deck that Q4’26 vs Q4’25 is +24%, in line with the platform
   +26% for the same period, by then, the anti‑import policies were already in
   effect in Q4’25

[image: image.png]



   -

   If we add CNLS into total CB, how will the YOY growth be like?

+22%, can see 2nd last row in the deck table


Slide 12 (Now Slide 11)

   -

   We fell behind for MY and PH? I thought we were always in front?

MY and PH was on par with TTS in Dec’25 but fell slightly behind from
Jan’26 to Feb’26, mainly due to 1) TTS didn’t increase base comm while SHP
mandatory fee increase significantly from Jan (MY CB FBS base comm
increased by 4% + 5% new mandatory ads to all CN sellers; PHCB FBS base
comm increased by 1.5%) and 2) TTS tends to spike more in Jan/Feb, slowing
down in later months


To catch up by Jun, we are accelerating FBS growth by setting more
aggressive targets for MY/PH and optimizing our existing FBS penetration
program. We will also grow total LFF seller base by running TTS 3PF seller
acquisition program.


Others

   -

   There is an initiative to acquire TTS only sellers via 3PFs? Can we
   bring it up, and where are we on it?


Yes, we’ve added this as slide 12

19d508dc9654f9b3.subject: [For JH, Ian Approval] [PHCB] Ads Auto Escrow Top Up
19d508dc9654f9b3.deck_filename: Shopee Mobile Mail - [For Vincent Approval] [CB] Ads Auto Escrow Top Up.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Jianghong, Ian,

We would like to seek your approval for PHCB to proceed with ads auto
escrow top up with seller consent prior to opt in.

Background: In Feb 2026, the CN government tightened the
implementation of stricter
online platform and antitrust rules, creating potential compliance risks.
In light of this, PH’s plan to auto opt in all sellers into the auto escrow
top up feature could violate these new regulations which prohibits
auto-enrollment in any program where sellers will incur additional costs.

Proposal: Initially planned for mid-March 2026 roll out, we will delay
PHCB’s implementation to June 2026. This will give us enough time to change
UI design for CNCB where we will explicitly need sellers’ consent prior to
opting them in.

Impact: Assuming lower adoption due to this change, we expect ads revenue
to be lower than what we currently have budgeted. FY26 PHCB ads rev will be
lower by -$578k (-0.03% of CB GMV, -0.004% of PLT GMV).

Kevin

Hi Jianghong and Ian,

Apologies for the late reply. *We are revisiting our plan with advice from
the CN MKT, CN Legal and Ads product teams. *

   - *PH already started the "auto-escrow" approach since February* of this
   year. Most CB sellers have already been on auto-escrow. (We did it batch by
   batch, so the remaining coverage and TR impact are small).
   - To switch to another mechanism adopted by other markets, *we likely
   need to first roll back the auto-escrow, and wait for a grace period*.

We are in close discussion with the teams mentioned above and will provide
a further update when we can confirm the steps and timeline.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

SIP


Slide 3

- for standalone p&l, we would need to adjust if it is indeed to PH
spending more? Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>


Per management guidance, SIP will spend more in PH for Mar-Jun as a
co-investment trial.

   -

   Mar target was set before the decision was finalized, resulting in a one-off
   mismatch and higher-than-expected Mar’26 target.
   -

   Apr-Jun targets have been updated to reflect the increased PH investment
   in the latest budget submission.


Table 1: SIP standalone P&L - PH import view (lower in Mar-Jun trial period)

[image: image.png]


- on ADO front, should we be overchieving if PH is spending more? Ailin Xie
<ailin.xie@shopee.com>


PH topline hit 100% RR in Mar, despite ~$70k additional spend.


The expected ADO uplift from this spend has not materialized yet, for 2
reasons:

Spend only started in the 2nd half of Mar to incubate new SKUs; new SKUs
   typically take 1-2 months to ramp up.

Ads CPO higher than BAU months as more spending shifted to non-ID lanes
   during Raya holiday, which have higher ads CPO vs ID lanes.


In Apr, as seasonal effects normalize and new SKUs mature, we expect +1.3k
ADO from $120k incremental spend (incremental CPO = $3). Overall topline
CB% +0.3pp vs Jan.


slide 9

- not much movement for #2 and #3? Ren Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>


Both were slow moving due to Voucher PM team was focusing on higher impact
P0 Key Projects till end April while both of our requests are P1 Key
Projects based on RX team assessment.


On #2 Direct seller voucher sync, PM team has not enough resources to
review BRD, delayed to mid April, we are pushing PM to start PRD right
after BRD confirmation. On #3 Local SIP seller voucher sync, we are asking
if PM can start PRD in early May after #2 PRD is done. Will follow up with
PM again by early next week.


slide 11

- for the lanes that have gone live, what is the ADO % of SKUs that have
gone live? Are we leaving anything on the table? Wei Qiang
<wei.qiang@shopee.com>


For BN lanes: 100% of SIP-MY SKU and ADO covered - All SIP-MY shops and
SKUs have been whitelisted to be available to BN buyers.


For VNKH/THLA lanes: ADO% of live SKUs is 67% for VNKH and 52% for THLA.
This is driven by 1) an “express opt-in” approach similar to MYBN, and 2)
fewer prohibited categories set by local PMO. Remaining gaps in VNKH/THLA
are largely structural (e.g., category limits, sellers submitted opt-out
form), with limited incremental onboarding upside.


slide 13

- has this been resolved? Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>


In progress. Legal is drafting updated TOS to obtain seller consent for
data transfer, ETA 9 Apr. Launch (start selling) on 22 Apr remains on track.


slide 14

- success rate seems low? Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>


THVN’s acceptance rate of 38% is above expectation. Local SIP typically
sees only 20-30% acceptance, due to it

- for each market, what is direct % of total export ADO?

In general, our
direct does not feel like it is growing much right now Joanne Lim Xinyi
<joanne.limxy@shopee.com> How Sai Kit <saikit.how@shopee.com>



MTD regionally Direct is* 20% of total export ADO (vs Jan export% +7pp, CB
penetration +1pp to 1.7%)*, and *28% total export ADG (vs Jan export%
+10pp, CB penetration +1.5pp to 2.8%)*. Growth in ADO share in all export
markets vs Jan; led by MY, ID, and TH.

   - MY Direct: 44% (+12pp vs Jan, +6.6k ADO)
   - ID Direct: 13% (+5pp, +3.7k)
   - TH Direct: 11% (+8pp, +1.3k)
   - VN Direct: 16% (+2pp, +0.4k)
   - PH Direct: 2% (+2pp, +80)
   - SG Direct: 46% (+19pp, +60)



I think we can do better for ID?
Probably need to be at VN scale?

Overall on track against the *Dec’26 targets of 23% export ADO and 33%
export ADG (65k ADO and $1M ADG)*.



Table 2: Direct selling share of export ADO/ADG by export market





others

- for overall SIP, how can we structurally go to sellers to get them to
reduce price or give us more revenue so we can reduce price for them?

i am
not sure we are going about this enough to make our overall skus the most
competitive, especially if we are doing the heavy lifting on behalf on the
sellers Ailin Xie <ailin.xie@shopee.com>



Structurally there are 3 levers to obtain seller investment

   - *Lever 1 - system features to replicate local shop investment (impact
   potential high, scalability high);* including,


   - *Seller discounted price*: 1) BAU discount - already live; 2) spike
      day discount (ETA Q3)
      - *Seller voucher*: highlighted above (ETA end Jun)
      - *Seller paid ads*: for sellers enrolled in local mandatory ads
      program, charge the same % escrow for their SIP orders (ETA end Jun)


   - *Lever 2 -  system features to co-share SIP operational cost with
   seller (impact potential high, scalability to be assessed)*


   - Return chats to seller (ETA Q3), planned pilot in AME lanes
      - Return RR cost to seller (ETA Q3), planned pilot in AME lanes


   - *Lever 3 - manual seller negotiation (impact potential low,
   scalability low)*


   - Trialled with ~5.7k top export sellers (50% of SIP ADO) - only 6%
      agreed to a 5–20% price reduction, yielding +826 normalised ADO.
      - Impact was limited by export team capacity constraints and low
      seller ownership of overseas growth.



For SIP sellers, what fees do they pay now?

Do they pay import or export
country fees?
Should we charge them?
And use it to reduce item price/vouchers etc?

Operationally they are not doing anything and still getting orders right
now.



As of now, we are *doubling down on Lever 1* for scalable impact, with
plans to *monitor the Lever 2 pilot* in AME markets.



Swarm



slide 2 -3

- do we have apr targets?

Jialin Tan <jialin.tan@shopee.com>



For Apr, we are on track to 1.8k ADO (+33% MoM), $24k ADG (+24% MoM);
seller count +93% MoM, SKU count +76% MoM.



UE targets -$1.03, lower than Mar as we have more new SKUs to incubate in
Apr.





slide 4

- what does leads mean?

it is more important to understand how many sellers
we are onboarding?
And how it compares vs March?

Jialin Tan
<jialin.tan@shopee.com>



*“Leads”* refers to sellers who expressed interest by submitting the Swarm
Onboarding Survey. There are 3 steps in Swarm seller funnel: Leads
(indicated interest) >> Onboarded sellers (opened shop) >> Active sellers
(selling with active listings). We are optimizing each step to improve
conversion to *active sellers*, which is the core acquisition KPI.



Active sellers increased from 372 (Mar) to 462 (Apr MTD),* +90 MoM*; *on
track to hit +265 new active sellers Apr target.* Growth is mainly driven
by more ERP partners and mass pitching.



Table 4: Swarm ERP seller acquisition performance Apr MTD across channels

others

- I remember we were going to optimize our spend on unique assortment vs
assortment in general. How is that coming along?

Yuanfeng Wu
<yuanfeng.wu@shopee.com>



Yes we have *shifted marketing spend and acquisition focus *to unique and
price competitive SKUs *since mid-Mar*.



Actualized P&L data for orders generated from mid-Mar started to come in
this week (~3 weeks lag time). We are in progress of analyzing data, to be
shared via email by EOW.



- for overall CN SIP in general, how is our ability to negotiate with
sellers and reduce selling cost overall?

Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>



Overall, *CNSIP’s ability to negotiate and reduce selling cost is weak*,
with a low pitching success rate of ~1.3% even during campaign periods.



This is mainly driven by structural constraints:

   - *Fragmented long-tail supply* - most sellers and SKUs are long-tail
   with low topline, limits collective bargaining power
   - *Derived settlement pricing* - settlement prices are estimated based
   on sellers’ other markets and may not align with seller expectations,
   creating understanding gap before negotiation can even begin
   - *Limited pitching resources* - heavy reliance on mass pitching, with
   minimal 1:1 engagement



As a result, *seller-level negotiation is not scalable*. Cost reduction is
better achieved through a *program-level pricing design (e.g., admin fees*).
However we believe the current priority is still on maximizing seller/SKU
supply, over revenue from sellers.



Does it make sense for SIP sellers who has been onboard for a while?

I
think we should start charging for our services especially for sellers who
have used it for a while. And use it to increase conversion/earn more P&L



In contrast,* Swarm achieves higher negotiation success due to its flexible
pricing logic*:

   - *Settlement price control *- Sellers can see and adjust settlement
   prices directly, allowing for easier price concessions.



How come this cannot be done for CB non-swarm sellers?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see findings for VNCB SOB drop.

For budget alignment to grow SOB, VNCB is pending VN FPnA alignment, ETA
7th April. We will work with VNCB team on this.

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB | MY & SG CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

Thanks!

Slide 6: Can we do SOB of SLS and LFF for this part?

Slide 7: How this "Only on Shopee/TTS Only" analysis done? Is this table
MECE or just based on sampled data?

Actually for this part, we are better than TTS for the "Only" part?

Slide 14: Should we aim at Oct 25 SOB as the baseline? 0.77?

Hi Jianghong,

Slide 6: Can we do SOB of SLS and LFF for this part?


Have included the SOB breakdown here. LFF is improving in SOB.

[image: image.png]


Slide 7: How this "Only on Shopee/TTS Only" analysis done? Is this table
MECE or just based on sampled data?

Actually for this part, we are better than TTS for the "Only" part?


Yes this is MECE and TTS data is based on the TTS crawling tool VN local
team uses. We are ahead for sellers exclusive to Shopee vs TTS but for
sellers that are on both platforms, we are behind especially for LFF
sellers.


Slide 14: Should we aim at Oct 25 SOB as the baseline? 0.77?

Yes, we aligned with VN to a more aggressive target at 0.77x SOB, based on
updated assumptions for TTS CB% (~7.40%), while keeping CPIGMV unchanged
(50%). Recovery (i.e. incremental ADG at ~480K) will come from growing VNCB
strength categories (ELHA, bulky Lifestyle), while managing prudent-defense
clusters (Fashion, FMCG) with disciplined SSC and value-led assortment to
capture demand from price-sensitive users.

Also, we are currently still pending budget approval from VN FP&A by 8th
April, the estimated budget needed is at $7.18M.

wrote:

*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

As we discussed yesterday, please do the following:

1) For SLS, can we adopt the same approach in TH to reinvest using SLS
profit.

2) For overall SOB target, let's aim at 0.77 and get approved by local as
soon as possible.

BTW, there are quite some outstanding issues need to get VN local support
for CNLS/CNCB. I'll ask Jina to set up a meeting with Anh and Saray early
next week.

Hi Ian

To give more visibility on the compulsory ads usage, please find the below

1. We track Ads Balance Depletion Ratio (weeks of coverage for ads
balance), and set Jan numbers (before compulsory ads) as target. In March,
the measures got worse, especially for sellers with take rate<5%.
[image: image.png]

2. The summary below outlines the levers we are acting on. The most
impactful ones are: (1) RM KPI for strong RM push as top 10 sellers
contribute to 20% of gap (2) Setting an expiry - we are in discussion with
relevant teams (PM, legal, finance)
[image: image.png]
3. Many of sellers with high balances actually have good  ROAS (benchmarked
agaisnt category ROAS) - we have identify the top sellers and are
pushing for more seller education with both RM push and MKT ads diagnosis
support
[image: image.png]

Best
Leo

content.answer:
Hi Ian

For your questions, pls find answers as follows:

Do we have a sense on what a good ratio would be?

First to clarify the number we are tracking is Ads days of coverage (which
I incorrectly communicated as weeks of coverage previously). Sincerely
apologize!


For CNCB MP total, we take pre compulsory-ads’ number (3-month average Nov’
25-Jan’ 26), as target for end of H1, which is 4.8 days (Mar actual is
8.7). However, this number can be very different across seller group. We
are closely monitoring as sellers ads-spending behaviors shift and plan to
have 7 days as safe line on seller level (given our weekly billing cycle)
once <5%TR sellers’ behavior start to stabilize.


Any sense why we saw a big increase in march for sellers with TR<5%?

It’s mostly due to under-spending of auto escrow - the increase in balance
is faster than the increase in spending, esp. for sellers who used to spend
very little or none. To avoid the snowball effect, we need to ensure
sellers’ net ads take rate above 4% (assuming 80% G2N and full transition
to Tech fee top-up), otherwise the DOC will keep accumulating.


Do we have a sense of overall ads escrow, is it increasing or?

The total top-up has decreased in March - mostly from >5% sellers reduce
their manual top-ups to prioritize consuming existing balance.

[image: image.png]

How do we deal with outliers? There will definitely be sellers who are not
spending and will keep accumulating?

There are a few types of outliers we’ve been monitoring

   -

   Strategic top brands <5% TR, i.e. PPMT and potentially Xiaomi in PH,
   which we have a special approval process to exclude them from auto-escrow
   which ultimately needs JH’s signoff.
   -

   Other managed sellers with hard complaints, we also have a process in
   place for RMs to file escalation, and so far we haven’t heard anything
   -

   For unmanaged sellers, potentially the more concerning ones, actions we
   are work

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 4

   -

   Where do we stand vs TT in terms of FBS/3PF penetration market by market?

Summary in slide 15, we are still behind TTS by 6%. We breakdown of how to
close is in late replies
[image: f3c12abb-f457-4985-9f6e-44fec64ed117.png]



   -

   What is the difference between FBS ADO % to seller and to shop?


Big CB sellers typically have multiple shops, such as multiple SLS shop +
multiple 3PF shop + 1 or multiple FBS shop.


We think tracking FBS% to seller help see seller as a whole and get them to
not only increase FBS shop penetration but also convert their SLS / 3PF
shop to FBS




   -

   Where did we land on when will FBS seller get FBS tag on SKU? What is
   the # of SKUs/ADO that will lose FBS tag? How are we getting these sellers
   to bring relevant variations into FBS? Is it an impactful initiative that
   we should work on?


For FBS tagging, we land on SG MY PH at 80% ADO threshold, TH at 60%
threshold. With this threshold, around ~50% SKU can retain its tag covering
~75% CB FBS ADO. This is expected to launch on 16 Apr. Around 50% of SKU
covering 25% ADO will lose the tag.


Will get FBS tag

SG

MY

TH

PH

#model coverage

45%

54%

55%

53%

ADO coverage

79%

73%

74%

71%


We’re working on a hunt list (for tag losing sellers with >70% ADO) and
will work with CN/KR to do a targeted push to encourage sellers to inbound
more models and prioritize the high performing models as low hanging fruit



Slide 7

   -

   I think we need to look at FBS ADO % of Shop level more closely, as
   compared to just SKUs


Yes, we also monitor ADO% of Shop (in slide 4, below please find the
summary)


In short, the seller penetration and shop penetration is growing in all
markets but we realise that SKU count is declining. Thus we deep dive to
SKU decline today, we will ensure


[image: image.png]



Slide 9

   -

   We calculated using S
increase addressable SKU,
Launch New SKU package,
Check WH capacity,

CN/KR to assess if to add to RM KPI


[image: image.png]



Slide 11

   -

   Have we looked at CR% assumptions at current ADIS requirement? First
   step should be to increase the CR%? Do we have market by market comparison?


Yes we look at CR% at the current addressable pool (factor in ADIS cut off
and licensing requirement). Current CR% is around 19% (PH/TH ADIS
requirement is more strict, hence pool is smaller and CR% higher at 27% and
19%)


Yes our proposal is to focus on current pool first and trigger ADIS
discussion when we hit >50% conversion of current pool



 [image: image.png]

Slide 14 -15

   -

   Do we have a sense of size of TTS only 3PF sellers by market? And what
   is the CR % that we expect to bring onboard?


We have a view of TTS only 3PF sellers based on 3PF survey, but we will
also gather this information from each local BI team, just like how we did
for VN.

Based on 3PF vendor survey, TTS only 3PF sellers total around 239k ADO
(slide 22), we are targeting to acquire ~20% of all sellers that 3PFs
indicated in the survey

   -

   For the gap between us and TTS right now, how much is it via acquisition
   of 3PF sellers we do not have, how much of it is increasing CR% of SLS

We will get back on this on more clear view with by market breakdown and by

for higher coverage over TTS only 3PF sellers pool Jason 21 Apr
